skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Reviews MERN stack projects for security, maintainability, performance, testing, architecture, and deployment readiness.
skill.summary: Structured MERN stack code review guidance with project health checks and framework-specific review references.
skill.author: 9tykeshav

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are primarily markdown examples and checklists for finding bad MERN code. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or executable payload was found, but the skill does instruct agents to inspect local files and run review commands, so publication should include a moderate tooling warning.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run local commands for project health checks, including TypeScript compilation, file-size scanning, dependency audit checks, and grep searches. These commands are aligned with code review work, but they should run only with user consent and in the intended repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present in the skill and are semantically tied to legitimate review tasks. Confidence is high for tool-use risk, but there is no evidence of malicious command construction or exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Repository File Inspection
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the agent to read project documentation, check package scripts, locate test directories, and inspect files by review scope. This is expected for a code reviewer, but it requires access to repository contents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly instructs repository inspection, which is necessary for its purpose. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because no sensitive file harvesting or hidden destination is described.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The many detections for eval, new Function, shell execution, localStorage, environment variables, weak crypto, and secret files occur in markdown checklists or bad/good examples. They describe patterns to detect in user projects rather than executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are markdown reference material and the suspicious patterns appear as explicit review examples. Confidence is very high that these static detections are false positives for executable malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Dependency Audit May Use Network Metadata
low_findings.description: The project health checklist includes checking npm audit issues. This can contact package advisory services or use dependency metadata, so users should understand that dependency data may leave the local machine depending on the command and environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is present and npm audit can involve network access in common environments. Confidence is medium because the skill does not force execution and exact behavior depends on the package manager configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Examples of Dangerous Patterns in Reference Material
dangerous_patterns.description: Reference files include vulnerable examples such as weak crypto, unsafe query patterns, localStorage token storage, dynamic code execution, and command injection. These examples are framed as bad patterns for reviewers to flag, not as behavior the skill should perform.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous strings are real, but their context is clearly educational code-review guidance. They should remain classified as examples, not confirmed malicious payloads.

content.value_statement: MERN reviews often miss deployment blockers, security flaws, and architecture risks. This skill provides a structured review workflow with targeted React, Express, Node.js, MongoDB, and full-stack references.
actual_capabilities: Guides project health checks before detailed code review.
Prioritizes findings by blockers, security, maintainability, performance, testing, and architecture.
Provides MERN-specific review checklists for React, Express, Node.js, MongoDB, and full-stack flows.
Defines a consistent review report format with severity groups and fix guidance.
Highlights common critical risks such as hardcoded secrets, missing auth, XSS, and unsafe MongoDB queries.
limitations: It is a guidance skill and does not include an automated scanner or executable analyzer.
Results depend on the files and commands the AI agent is allowed to inspect.
It focuses on MERN projects and may not fit unrelated stacks.
Reference material includes example vulnerable code that should be read as review guidance only.

use_cases.title: Pre-release MERN Audit
use_cases.description: Review a repository before deployment to identify build blockers, missing security controls, large files, and testing gaps.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Inspect changed React, Express, Node.js, or MongoDB files and produce prioritized findings with concrete fixes.

use_cases.title: Architecture Health Check
use_cases.description: Assess maintainability, module boundaries, data access patterns, and operational readiness in a growing MERN codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Review One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused file
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this MERN file for correctness, security, maintainability, and performance. Use the skill output format and prioritize serious issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Changed files in a feature branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changed files in this pull request. Check project health briefly, then focus on security, regressions, tests, and maintainability risks introduced by the change.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full MERN repository review. Start with build status, docs, tests, file sizes, and dependencies, then sample key frontend, backend, and database files.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Security Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a security-focused MERN review. Inspect auth, token storage, CORS, input validation, MongoDB query safety, file uploads, secrets handling, and error disclosure.

output_examples.input: Review a MERN pull request that changes login and user profile APIs.
output: Project health summary with build, tests, blockers, and large files.
Critical findings for missing auth checks or unsafe token handling.
Important findings for validation, error handling, and missing tests.
A deployment verdict and a suggested order for fixes.

output_examples.input: Review a React dashboard component for performance and maintainability.
output: Scope statement for the reviewed component.
Findings on component size, hook usage, memoization, rendering cost, and accessibility.
Concrete remediation steps ordered by user impact and risk.

output_examples.input: Audit MongoDB and Express code before production launch.
output: Security findings for query injection, missing middleware, weak CORS, and secrets exposure.
Performance findings for missing indexes, N+1 queries, and blocking operations.
A clear blocked, needs fixes, or ready verdict.

best_practices: Run the project health phase before reading individual implementation files.
Load reference files only when a specific MERN pattern needs verification.
Report findings with file locations, severity, impact, and a concrete fix path.
anti_patterns: Do not claim a repository has no issues without inspecting relevant files and checks.
Do not treat style preferences as critical defects.
Do not load every reference file upfront when the review scope is narrow.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for MERN applications using MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js.

faq.question: Does the skill execute code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It gives instructions to the AI agent, which may run review commands when permitted by the user environment.

faq.question: What commands might it suggest?
faq.answer: It suggests commands such as TypeScript checks, file-size scans, dependency audits, and grep searches.

faq.question: Can it review a single file?
faq.answer: Yes. The scope calibration section supports single-file, pull request, feature, and full repository reviews.

faq.question: Does it replace a security scanner?
faq.answer: No. It complements scanners with contextual code review guidance and manual reasoning.

faq.question: Why are there vulnerable code snippets in the references?
faq.answer: They are examples of patterns to flag during review, not instructions to execute vulnerable code.
